text: Always prefer mage commands over raw go commands. Mage targets ensure consistent builds with version injection and proper flags. Key targets: mage build (bop binary), mage buildMCP (bop-mcp binary), mage buildAll (both), mage test, mage testRace, mage testCoverage, mage testUnit, mage testVerbose, mage format, mage lint, mage lintFix, mage vet, mage ci (full CI: format+lint+test-race+build-all), mage check (quick pre-commit: format+vet+test), mage all (everything), mage deps, mage tidy, mage generate, mage install, mage clean. Fallback raw go commands: go build -o bop ./cmd/bop, go build -o bop-mcp ./cmd/bop-mcp, go test ./..., go test -race ./..., gofmt -w ., golangci-lint run.

Running reviews: ./bop review branch main (review current branch against main), ./bop review branch main --reviewers security,architecture (with specific personas), ./bop review branch main --output ./review-output (with output directory), ./bop review branch main --instructions 'Focus on security' (with custom context). GitHub Action usage: uses: delightfulhammers/bop/action@v0.12.3 with anthropic-api-key from secrets.

Core development rules: (1) TDD is mandatory — write tests first. (2) Clean Architecture — domain has no external dependencies. (3) Functional style — prefer immutability, SOLID principles. (4) Always use mage — prefer mage commands over raw go commands. (5) Fix ALL lint errors — fix any lint errors you encounter, even if you didn't cause them. This codebase accumulated technical debt from ignoring pre-existing issues; the only way to recover is to fix issues as you find them.

Definition of Done checklist: (1) Tests written (TDD). (2) Code formatted (mage format). (3) Lint passes (mage lint) — fix ALL errors, not just yours. (4) All tests pass (mage test). (5) Build succeeds (mage buildAll). (6) No race conditions (mage testRace).

Common pitfalls: (1) Don't skip tests — TDD is mandatory. (2) Don't import domain from adapters — clean architecture violation. (3) Don't commit secrets — redaction exists but prevention is better. (4) Don't use raw go commands — use mage targets instead. (5) Don't ignore the race detector — mage testRace must pass. (6) Don't ignore lint errors — fix ALL errors you find, even pre-existing ones.

PR Triage Protocol: Use bop MCP server tools for triage. The server exposes tools in two categories. Reading: list_annotations (SARIF), get_annotation, list_findings (PR comments), get_finding, get_thread, get_code_context, get_diff_context, get_suggestion. Actions: reply_to_finding, post_comment, mark_resolved, request_rereview, edit_finding, post_findings. Triage workflow: (1) List findings via list_annotations + list_findings (always check both). (2) Analyze via get_finding, get_code_context, get_suggestion. (3) Fix valid findings in code. (4) Respond via reply_to_finding for PR comments, post_comment for SARIF. (5) Resolve via mark_resolved. (6) Re-review via request_rereview after fixes are pushed.

Additional documentation beyond the core docs: Design docs: docs/design/ (active design docs including 02-ARCHITECTURE.md). Archive: docs/archive/ (44 historical docs — old TDDs, roadmaps, phase designs, implementation plans). Security: docs/SECURITY.md (security model and threat analysis). GitHub Action Setup: docs/GITHUB_ACTION_SETUP.md (action integration guide). Configuration: docs/CONFIGURATION.md (config file reference). Context: docs/context/ (Telesis-managed context).

Claude Code skills for targeted context loading: /skill development (building, testing, debugging), /skill github-workflow (commits, PRs, issues), /skill architecture (design questions, understanding codebase), /skill review (using the code reviewer itself), /skill triage-pr-review (triage PR code review feedback and SARIF alerts). Use these skills for targeted context instead of reading docs manually.

Project status: v0.12.3 stable. Phase 3.5 (Local Mode) is complete. Key completed phases: Phase 1 (Foundation) — multi-provider LLM, local CLI, basic GitHub workflow. Phase 2 (GitHub Native) — first-class reviewer with inline annotations. Phase 3.1 (Triage) — MCP server for AI-assisted triage. Phase 3.2 (Personas) — specialized reviewer roles. Phase 3.5 (Local Mode) — on-demand PR review, session storage, MCP review tools. Phase 3.3 (Dynamic Model Selection) was deferred. Interactive TUI (Bubble Tea) was planned but never implemented and is effectively dropped.
